name: Chili Con Carne
recipeIngredient: 1 cup Chopped peppers (of your choice, optional)
recipeInstructions:
Yield: 9 pints
Procedure:
1. Wash beans thoroughly and place them in a 2-quart saucepan.
2. Add cold water to a level of 2 to 3 inches above the beans and soak for 12 to 18 hours.
3. Drain and discard water.
4. Combine beans with 5-1/2 cups of fresh water and 2 teaspoons salt. Bring to a boil.
5. Reduce heat and simmer until tender, about 30 minutes.
6. Drain and discard water.
7. Brown ground beef, chopped onion, and peppers (if desired) in a skillet. Drain off fat.
8. Add salt, pepper, chili powder, tomatoes, and drained cooked beans to the skillet. Simmer for 5 minutes.
9. Fill jars, leaving 1-inch headspace. Adjust lids and process according to Table 1 or Table 2.
Table 1. Recommended processing time for Chili Con Carne in a dial-gauge pressure canner.
Style of Pack: Hot. Jar Size: Pints.
Processing Time: 75 minutes
Canner Pressure (PSI) at Altitudes of 0 - 2000 ft: 11 lb
2001 - 4000 ft: 12 lb
4001 - 6000 ft: 13 lb
6001 - 8000 ft: 14 lb
Table 2. Recommended processing time for Chili Con Carne in a weighted-gauge pressure canner.
Style of Pack: Hot. Jar Size: Pints.
Processing Time: 75 minutes
Canner Pressure (PSI) at Altitudes of 0 - 1000 ft: 10 lb
Above 1000 ft: 15 lb
